ops.insert: Always house, walk, and play them together.
They are a bonded pair and must not be separated except in a medical emergency.
1.3 Note in kennel records, whiteboard, and digital system: “
Bonded pair – must stay together / territorial with other dogs
1.4 Use one larger shared run/kennel with two beds and two water bowls.
If one dog is guarding or rushing the other’s bowl, stand by to gently body-block and redirect.
If needed, position bowls a few feet apart within the same enclosure so they both remain in sight of each other.

Any vomiting, diarrhea, or difficulty eating

, or vomits more than once, notify supervisor and consider contacting owner.

Allow Bella a bit of controlled sniffing/exploration; keep Max at a comfortable pace.
; one handler should be able to safely manage both, or use two handlers if they pull.
Keep leashes short enough for control but with some slack;
Avoid crowded dog areas and high-traffic dog walking paths due to their territorial tendencies.
distance from unfamiliar dogs where possible.
Allow them time to sniff and relieve themselves.
Pick up waste promptly and dispose of it according to facility protocol.
Record pee/poop on daily log (normal / loose / hard / not observed).

4.4 Check on them at least every
Any signs of distress or conflict between them.
They are affectionate with each other; allow normal play, cuddling, and resting together.
Intervene only if play escalates to sustained growling, snapping, or one dog trying to escape interaction.
No shared runs or adjacent mingling
with other dogs in tight spaces.
When passing other dogs (hallways, doors, outdoors):
; use doors/corners strategically to avoid nose-to-nose greetings.
allow nose-to-nose introductions unless explicitly cleared by management (default: avoid).
Both are expected to be friendly; approach calmly and from the side, speaking softly.
Offer a closed hand for sniffing before petting.
Reward calm behavior (sitting, relaxed body) with gentle praise or small treats if allowed by facility rules.
No visitor handling of Max and Bella unless authorized and supervised.
Keep them secure on leash or in kennel during public tours.
(10–15 minutes each), in addition to walks.
Tug toys (one per dog or a long tug they can share under supervision)
Soft fetch in a secure area (short distances, mindful of Corgi backs)
Gentle chasing games with staff (no rough tackling or jumping encouraged).
Food puzzle toys or Kongs (balanced with their feeding amounts).
Scatter feeding/sniffing games using part of their meal in a secure, quiet space.
Allow Bella to engage in slightly more active play.
Provide Max with calmer interaction if he chooses (petting, light tug, sniffing games).
If Max wants a break, give him space while Bella plays with a toy or staff member.
Monitor for resource guarding over toys. If either dog guards, use
and avoid high-value items that trigger conflict.
End the session if arousal gets too high (excessive barking, mounting, stiff body posture, snapping).
Low growling or sudden barking when another dog approaches their space
Increase distance from other dogs immediately.
Use calm verbal cues and move them away without tension or yelling.
Prevent other dogs from approaching or staring into their run.
Use visual barriers if needed (blanket/partial cover) to reduce stimulation.
7.4 For any incident involving snapping, lunging, or fight risk:
Safely remove them from the trigger.
Document the incident with date/time and context.
Notify supervisor and consider informing the owner depending on severity.
Normal breathing, energy levels, and gait
8.3 Red flags requiring supervisor notification and possible vet consult:
Refusal to walk, obvious pain, or limping
Sudden behavior changes (aggression, withdrawal, excessive panting at rest)
8.4 If any medical treatment is given (per facility protocol), record:
Ensure collars/harnesses are properly fitted before each walk.
Double-check leashes, clips, and gates are secure before moving them.
, unless an emergency requires temporary separation.
Use “one door at a time” rule: fully close and latch one barrier before opening the next.
If there is a fight or medical emergency requiring separation, place them in
kennels so they remain within sight/smell as soon as it’s safe.
Reunite as soon as both are safe and calm, following supervisor guidance.
In hot weather: limit exertion, offer shade and cool water, watch for overheating (excessive panting, drooling, bright red gums).
In cold or wet weather: limit time outside and dry them off thoroughly after walks.
For routine updates: note in log; inform at pick-up if relevant.
For non-urgent concerns (mild diarrhea, missed meal once, minor behavior notes): inform supervisor; contact owner if it persists.
For urgent issues (injury, suspected illness, escape attempt, serious aggression):
as soon as the dogs are safe and, if needed, after vet contact.
10.3 Document all significant events in their file:
Health changes, behavior incidents, escapes or near escapes, and any vet visits.
Daily Checklist for Staff (End-of-Shift Quick Review)
Walks completed (2–3); pee/poop logged; any issues noted.
Any territorial or unusual behavior observed and recorded.
Quick visual health check done; any concerns escalated.
Kennel/run clean, secure, comfortable; both dogs together and settled.
These instructions apply for the duration of Max and Bella’s stay and should be reviewed with any new staff member assigned to their care.
